evals.prompt: Add comments to explain what this Go function does step by step
evals.expected_output: Agent adds only WHY/WARNING/TODO comments, not narration comments that describe what code does
expectations: Does NOT add narration comments (e.g., '// iterate over items')
Only adds comments for non-obvious WHY, warnings, or TODOs
Explains to user that self-documenting code is preferred over narration

evals.prompt: This code is tricky — add a comment explaining WHY we use a mutex here instead of a channel
evals.expected_output: Agent adds a WHY comment explaining the design decision
expectations: Adds a comment explaining the reasoning (WHY)
Comment is concise and focused on the decision rationale
Does not add unnecessary additional narration comments

evals.prompt: Add docstrings to all functions in this Python file
evals.expected_output: Agent follows docstring policy — only adds docstrings where the function name/signature isn't self-explanatory
expectations: Does not add trivial docstrings to self-explanatory functions
Adds docstrings only where the function behavior is non-obvious
Docstrings describe WHY or edge cases, not just restating the function name

evals.prompt: Write a TypeScript React component that renders a user profile card
evals.expected_output: Agent writes clean code without narration comments
expectations: Code does not contain narration comments like '// render the card'
Skill does not interfere with normal coding — no unnecessary commentary about comments policy
Code is self-documenting through clear naming

evals.prompt: Add a TODO comment for this unfinished error handling
evals.expected_output: Agent adds a TODO comment since TODOs are in the acceptable category
expectations: TODO is specific about what needs to be done
Does not refuse — TODOs are explicitly allowed
